Details: Ion exchange is a one of the Best Available Technologies for chromium (III) and chromium (VI) removal.  Ion exchange can effectively remove chromium (III) (68 to 100 percent) and chromium (VI) (95 to 100 percent) under favorable water quality conditions. The cationic resins have shown promising results for chromium (III) removal whereas anionic resins work best for chromium (VI) removal (based on the literature cited here).  Removal efficiency can vary based upon the choice of resin, regeneration frequency, competing ions, and their concentrations, initial chromium (III) and or chromium (VI) concentrations, influent pHs and contact time. The popular resins in literature used for chromium (III) and chromium (VI) removal includes zeolite, zeolite modified with iron, cellulose based ion exchange resins, strong base anion exchange rasins and weak base anion exchange resins.
